module.title: SAP TM Organizational Structure & Master Data

topic.title: SAP TM Organizational Model - Best Practices & Real-World Implementation

learningObjectives.title: Understand organizational model implementation
learningObjectives.description: Explain how to design and roll out organizational structures for transportation planning.

learningObjectives.title: Apply best practices
learningObjectives.description: Use scalable, business-driven planning organizations and responsibility rules.

learningObjectives.title: Identify implementation pitfalls
learningObjectives.description: Recognize common mistakes and governance risks in real-world projects.

sections.content: Designing an effective Organizational Model is one of the most important implementation activities in SAP TM. A well-designed model improves planner productivity, enables automatic responsibility determination and supports scalable transportation operations.

sections.title: Implementation Principle
sections.content: Design the Organizational Model based on business processes rather than organizational charts. Transportation responsibilities should reflect operational needs.

items: Reuse organizational objects across transportation scenarios.

sections.content: A multinational automotive manufacturer operates in 12 countries. Instead of maintaining one central planning team, the company creates regional Planning Organizations for Asia, Europe and North America. Responsibility determination automatically routes Freight Units to the correct planning team based on Transportation Zone and Plant.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Keep responsibility rules simple, scalable and business-driven. Complex rule sets become difficult to maintain and troubleshoot.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A retail company reduced transportation planning time by 35% after replacing manual planner assignment with automatic responsibility determination based on Transportation Zones and Shipping Points.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Implementation interviews often ask how you would design Planning Organizations for a multinational company. Explain the design using regions, transportation modes and business units rather than individual users.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Avoid creating Planning Organizations for every planner. Planning Organizations should represent business responsibility, while planners belong to Planning Groups.

items: Organizational design should follow business processes.
Use automatic responsibility determination whenever possible.
Simple organizational models are easier to maintain and scale.
